name: Docusaurus
what: Static site generator for docs, blogs, and knowledge sites using React/Markdown/MDX.
solves: Public documentation portals, help centers, knowledge sites, and documentation-driven content publishing.
users: Engineering, docs, and content teams who need a versionable docs site.
fit: Useful if Hylono wants a separate docs/help site, but the public website is already Next.js 16 and server-first. Docusaurus is optional, not default.
runtime: Node-based build tool that emits static assets; hosting is simple once built.
components: Build system, markdown/MDX content, React theme/components, search integrations, and static hosting.
deploy: Low operational burden. The bigger question is whether it duplicates the existing Next.js site’s content capabilities.
deps: Node/npm, static hosting or CDN, optional search provider, docs-authoring workflow.
auth: None by default; docs can be public or protected behind external hosting patterns.
security: Mostly content/hosting security. Much lower ops burden than dynamic apps.
setup_path: Only use if Hylono wants a separate docs property. Otherwise keep docs/content inside the existing Next.js site.
env: Build-time config, analytics/search keys, base URL, deployment target settings.
persistence: Version control is the main persistence model.
operations: Content workflows and search/indexing updates matter more than infrastructure.
failure_points: Creating a second frontend/docs stack without strong reason.
integration: Can coexist as a separate docs site, but the current Next.js stack may already be sufficient for SEO-heavy documentation.
verify: Decide whether a separate docs site is strategically better than keeping docs within the main Next.js architecture.
risk: Low infrastructure risk, moderate duplication risk.
gdpr: Minimal direct risk unless analytics/comment systems are added.
recommendation: OPTIONAL / ONLY IF SEPARATE DOCS PROPERTY IS WANTED
overlap: Overlaps with existing Next.js content stack and partly with BookStack/Outline for documentation.
codex_read_first: Read the current content architecture before proposing Docusaurus.
codex_best_path: Default to the existing Next.js site unless there is a specific reason for a separate docs platform.
codex_never_assume: Never assume another React docs framework is automatically a win just because it is popular.
